Observation Details: This morning Emma felt well enough to join on the birding walk we had booked. We managed to have the lodge arrange for a staff member to watch Maddie for the morning so we could both bird baby free! I think they would not want to do that again as she kinda took over the small balcony restaurant which is also the kitchen and office. Anyways, we hiked to the car and then drove to the entry overlook where we met our guide. Slow start but then got a mixed flock over the ridge road and a few mountain tanagers. I spotted this bird and glimpsed just tiny bit of buff color to be excited that it could be a sought lifer. Got good looks as it crept though tangle of bamboo and vines. Enjoyed the look first, then took some terrible obscured pics.

Observation Details: Flew by as we stood on the observation tower.  When shadows are removed from this shot it shows that the underwings are blue (thanks to Don for the suggestion).  Both Merlin and INaturalist ID's as Bronze-winged Parrot and Don concurred.  Hoping someone else may have a better shot to add.

Media notes: Las dos primeras fotos pueden ser el mismo ejemplar, ya que fueron tomadas en lugares cercanos el uno del otro. La última de las fotos, la estructura de bambú, puede mostrar un individuo diferente, pero no tengo certeza de si son dos o se trata solamente de uno. El ave no estaba en una jaula, la malla corresponde al cerco perimetral del ecoparque.
